title: Order of automations on a board

posts.author: Anja (Anja Dijkstra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
would it be possible to group the automations in the overview window by dragging them or changing their order?
I have a lot on one board and they are now in order of adding them, which makes it hard to check which automations belong together.
Also I am not sure if the order can cause a conflict or if this is sorted in the background.
e.g.
automation 1 ‘when status a changes move pulse to another board’ and followed by automation 2 ‘when status a changes - set the date in column x’

so it would be nice to change the order around so the automation makes more sense
many thanks
Anja

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Anja! Great questions here. Our automations team is working hard to add more functionality to the automations store including the ability to prioritize automations and change ownership. Changing ownership is our first tackle and then we will move onto other ways to optimize automations in general on your boards. Stay tuned for these developments coming soon! Cheers!

posts.author: dkoviessen (Darren Koviessen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would also love to have control the order in which automations are run…or have Monday automatically sort them depending on the type (e.g. move to a new board as the last automation executed). Noticed that some automatins fail to run properly because of this.

Thunderbird is actually a good example of an application with this type of system working correctly. It allows you to have filters listed in any order but executes them in a certain order and shows you order as well.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lauralev. If changing ownership is the first tackle where does dynamic (build-your-own) automations fall in the roadmap? From other topics I thought this was the next priority with automations.

lauralev:

Changing ownership is our first tackle

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml: I think a lot of people seem to be finding that updates made prior to ‘move item to board’ automations are hit and miss because it’s not always the last automation to run. The ideal scenario is automation ordering, but in the short term If you could add a default pause just on board move automations so they are ALWAYS the last automation to run you’ll solve a big issue. At the moment I populate a date field then move a pulse, I also have the automation in the next board too as a fallback which support recommended but even then it’s still hit and miss. Super frustrating.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Krishele! Great question - it is our first tackle in terms of managing automations as a whole not specific recipes. Dynamic recipes is also up there on our roadmap as well. Stay tuned for all the developments to come!

posts.author: Jack (Jack Stirk)
posts.cookedHtml: All that would be needed to fix this i’d imagine would be a ‘When status changes to x, move to item to board, after x days’ automation… You can do it with a group move already but just need the same with a board move.

posts.author: thisisadvantage (Sam Petrusma)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 to this, we have a bunch of automations taking place that would perform drastically different depending on the order run. Any ETA?

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @thisisadvantage - we don’t have an ETA yet, but thank you for sharing your support for this feature request!

posts.author: mmeneses (Matt Meneses)
posts.cookedHtml: Just following up on this post. It would be great to set automation priorities because they’re like the wild west right now. Notification automations with dynamic text are my biggest culprit. People will get notifications with dates or statuses as blanks which removes the value of the notification.

posts.author: HilaryO (Hilary Osterman)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a really good suggestion - I’m disappointed that it hasn’t been done as it would fix the problem!

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @lauralev @melissav-monday.com is there any update on this? It would be really useful to be able to drag automations to change their order.

posts.author: MatthewM (Matthew Mathis)
posts.cookedHtml: This is also something my team and I are interested in. At the very least, having an understanding of how they are executed would be helpful also. Is there any information available as to how Monday determines the order in which to execute automations on a board?

posts.author: aviation22 (Richard Holme)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any further update on this? It looks like it’s been in the works for over a year.

I have a list of automations creating sub-items and I need them to appear in a specific order. It would be good to be able to move the order around in the automations interface.

posts.author: Tesa (T Stoodley)
posts.cookedHtml: in addition to when they trigger, I would like to arrange them to depict a process document, rather than having to recreate that document somewhere else.

posts.author: zposen (Zane Posen)
posts.cookedHtml:
@melissav-monday.com @lauralev any update on this? It is completely defeating the purpose of our automation stack to not be able to execute automations in a custom order. Looks like this has been in the works for close to three years at this point.

Thanks.

posts.author: BeaCruz (Beatriz Cruz)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
I am facing problems with my automations because they do not have a correct order. It could be very simple as change position of automation inside of automation´s board

posts.author: eli (Eli Schweyer)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need this feature. I use “create a subitem when an item is created” feature and I want to be able to organize the order of the subitems (like a daily routine) but the subitems are created in the order they are listed on the automation center. Being able to drag the order of automations would make my life so much easier and save SO much time!

posts.author: V_Szabi (Szabolcs Vincze)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @lauralev !
Changing the sequence of integrations and automations would be very useful in my work.
How did you manage to create that on the platform since then?

Szabi

posts.author: Vtab (Vero Tabares)
posts.cookedHtml:
Custom notifications with merge fields are sort of useless when the notification is being fired off before all of the item data has been added to the board (if the item is created from another automation). My notifications include blank fields that add no value.

I can’t think of a workaround aside from a generic “A new item has been created” message.

posts.author: Michelleatpm (Michelle Berrisford)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this? I would love the ability to rearrange automations, and the active workflows maybe folders or something so I’m not shifting through 40-80 automations on a board to trouble shoot items?

posts.author: mkingsland (Morgan Kingsland)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! I’d like to be able to rearrange the automations in each board in the board automation view.

Sometimes I create an automation that’s near the end of the workflow before creating one at the beginning of the workflow and would like to rearrange them so that I can see the automation workflow in its entirety and be sure I’m not missing anything.

Thanks!

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
mkingsland:

automations in ea

I think I understand your recommendation … does your recommendation include arranging / filtering automations? For example, I have 37 automations in this board and Find it difficult to see if I have an automation as I scroll up and down looking for it. Hope that your recommendation means to be able to arrange / filter this screen. If NOT I’d like to expand your recommendation to having a feature that does that

image1154×387 38.2 KB

posts.author: mkingsland (Morgan Kingsland)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I was asking about arranging in particular, but filtering is a great addition!!

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! Thanks so much for writing a post about this. We are working on more functionalities in the automations store and I’ve forwarded your request onto our design team to take a look. Stay tuned for some changes… If you have any other thoughts - feel free to continue sharing!

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: I also would suggest the ability to name each automation. This way when you have many of them (and frequently they appear identical), you don’t have to click in each one to see what that automation is. If they could be named (labeled) such as “Client x automation to…” it would be a huge time saver and make the identification of each automation easier.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Love this suggestion!! I’ve ended up with similar pulses being created because I had two reoccurring automations for the same task.

DF2019:

frequently they appear identical)

posts.author: Schellekens (Patrice)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes it s a good idea, i propose a same view as mindmap.

posts.author: anon34162818 (anon34162818)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be a very useful option! A definite necessity in the future…managing integrations and automation’s takes a lot of time if you cannot organize them, move them and sort them.

posts.author: don_gummow (Don Gummow)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this, I would really appreciate the ability to name automations for better organization. As I have tried to champion the use of automations within my team, one of the obstacles is that they can become disorganized quickly without the ability to name them, and this actually discourages people from using the feature.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Don, good point. Too many automations without being able to sort or name them causes confusion and a big waste of time trying to figure out which is which.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for the great announced progress in this area today!!! @lauralev

The below agree and give some added context to this idea -

  • The amazing way that searches work on boards would be helpful in the automations window
  • Sorting would put all automations that are named similarly in groups, and even organize the columns named in them as well
  • Filtering would be ultimate (just like on the board)
  • Naming would be a nice to have at that point, but I would suggest naming is a tag or metadata field on the automation, not changing the way it appears on the screen. I rather like seeing the full automation (except notification details) right in front of me and wouldn’t want renaming to cover up that good info.
    -moving/copying automations from board to board is the biggest need though @lauralev!!! When we optimize our process - having to rebuild an automation on each board that is already live is just another manual step that burdens us (like adding a column to every board, updating column settings or permissions on every board… etc etc). I think this functionality is critical to increased adoption from all customers

posts.author: jason.clark (jason clark)
posts.cookedHtml: @mkingsland I’m looking for the same added feature, integrations too! Thanks for bringing it up. There should be a way to drag and drop, similar to a pulse, to rearrange the order in the view.

posts.author: kare (Kåre Kabel Mai)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a really welcome feature. Has it been implemented already? - this thread hasn’t been updated for a while…

posts.author: bcoon (Brian Coon)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be amazing if this added to monday.com functionality. Some of our boards are really advanced and we need good visibility of the automations so that we can understand the functionality of the board. The boards can be hard to understand as it is.

Brian;

posts.author: Ian1 (Ian Burgess-Simpson)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be incredibly useful. I go cross eyed trying to read through my automations and relate them to each other. I’d like to add another feature request that if you write a duplicate of an automation you already have on a board it will tell you. I find I sometimes forget I already covered something and I write it again. partly because you can’t order the automations in a logical sequence to review your overall construction

posts.author: favioiraola (Favio De Iraola)
posts.cookedHtml: I would really appreciate if you allow users to manage automations in any given board, lets say sort by type, a-z, z-a, date, on/off, or arbitrary named groups in order to make easier to navigate through what may become 10s or 100s of automations rules.

posts.author: jfoust (Jennifer Foust)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to move automations into step by step order would be incredibly helpful. I’m currently using the “+ Add description” field to include the step number and/or a brief description, but when the board becomes more complex, and the automations order the most currently edited item to the top, it gets messy quickly. I’ve literally copied and pasted the automations into Word, cut them apart, and put them in order manually to check layouts. I’ve even typed everything into Excel and moved the rows as needed. It’s needlessly time consuming, but for me it’s the only way to easily walk through the automation flow.

posts.author: etienneoh (Etienne Ouellette-Halle)
posts.cookedHtml:
A way to organize automations would be incredible ! Just a way to find automations easier when there is over 30 automations in some of our boards.
It could be in the form of a simple folder that you can unfold, nothing over the top. Also, the description which can be added to automations should have more space since a short sentence doesn’t fit.

posts.author: mdefields (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is this ever going to done? I have 52 automations on one board and REALLY need to be able to order them as I need, not just as they were built or updated.
Thank you!
Michelle

posts.author: kailyirons-fuda (Kaily Irons-Fuda)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love a feature to custom-order automations. I imagine having them auto-sort in order of execution would be difficult since every board flow is different, but it would be extremely helpful if we could custom arrange them to make it easy to follow which automations are in order and change/update them when necessary.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is still in need from our side also. It is really annoying that automations trigger unreliably or interfere with one another because of the random order in which they are executed.
Despite automations having evolved quite a bit there are still cases where I cannot put the entire process in one automation because I cannot define “OR”-triggers.

For example I have an automation that replaces a person when a status changes and another automation that adds another person under certain conditions. The conditions differ, so I cannot put them in the same automation.
Now when the automation to replace is executed last the person that should have been added in the second step will be replaced as well. But I need the first person from the first automation to replace whoever is assigned prior to the two automations. Meaning I cannot simply have both persons added. Only the second one.

posts.author: Rosco (Rosco)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have only three automations on my board. The random nature of the firing order and triggers causes complete chaos. Coupled with weakest set of dependencies integration ever, I’m seriously considering moving back to using spreadsheets.
How are all these issues open for years?

posts.author: MortenN (Morten Nielsen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rosco,

If I understand your issue correctly, you need your automations to run in the same order consistently. One way to ensure this, would be adding an extra status column, and then adding an “…and only if is ”. Then make sure that this status is changed to the next step at the end of the automation.
For the next automation it could trigger on this status column change (for steps) and also check if your other condition is met.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: klewis (Karli Lewis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is a big issue for me as well. I need to execute automations in a particular order. It’s not prioritizing my automations in a sensible way, which is causing some issues with notifications and moving items around between groups.

Do we foresee this happening any time soon? This thread is quite old.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Cody,

The new workflow is getting better and better. For me it does not support app actions blocks (apps that publishes the action). Do you know if that is in the making? Until that is not supported I would not recommend our customers to use the new workflow.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, if users have need of integrations and third-party app automations, then workflow builder doesn’t support those today. I do not have anything I can share (I don’t work for monday) with details beyond I would expect its going to come eventually.

But if someone has a workflow that is doable with just built-in automations, then its starting to be usable.

I was more sharing the existence in response to this thread so people know that no, monday.com isn’t just ignoring this request but is actively working on the solution and has it in beta.

posts.cookedHtml: If you really want it, reach out to support@monday.com and ask nicely. I can’t promise any results (I don’t work for monday.com) but it isn’t impossible it might help either.

posts.author: tfVu8 (Your full name)
posts.cookedHtml: Why doesn’t Monday have automation ordering? Maybe I should cancel my company’s subscription.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: As mentioned above, its coming, and in beta.

posts.author: vbickov (Volodymyr Bykov)
posts.cookedHtml:
anon29275264:

, its coming, and in beta.

Will we be able to see the list automations in a particular constant order in the UI as well? This is highly annoying that the list of automations is constantly changing. Since automations are obviously better than pretty weak workflows, the custom order would be very useful.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is a strict graph of trigger, conditions, and actions in a view. Each workflow has its own view akin to a board - it exists outside of a single board because now the workflows can CROSS boards. Each workflow is defined as a condition/action graph. So you now have condition is true, and actions, and condition is false - a different set of actions being possible.

So yes, everything is now self contained for one workflow without clutter from anything else.

Just beware that it may be a paid/enterprise feature once release. I wouldn’t dream too hard on it if you’re not on at least a pro account.

posts.author: JoseDaher (Jose Daher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah! That would be awsome… To be able to group and/or drag them to rearrange.

Hope it get implemented.

posts.author: Warren_Miller (Warren)
posts.cookedHtml:
We would love this to be a feature too. either by numbering the order in which automations fire, or allowing us to tie automations to be tied into a timeline.

Ie " When time tracking is 01:00, notify person" to allow previous automations to have taken action.

We would also love the ability to group automations, copy and paste automations too.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
To make it easier to manage your automations, we can implement a drag-and-drop feature in the overview window. This will allow you to group related automations together visually, improving organization and readability. We’ll also add functionality to reorder automations by dragging them, ensuring they are listed in a sequence that makes logical sense for your workflow.

By grouping and reordering automations, you’ll be able to:

  • Easily identify which automations belong together
  • Minimize potential conflicts by ensuring dependent automations follow a logical order
  • Simplify maintenance and updates of your automation workflows

posts.cookedHtml: How close is this to becoming a real item we can use? Please advise ASAP @melissav-monday.com

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team. Thank you for sharing your thoughts on the order of automations. We wanted to clarify the two main requests we’ve received here:

  1. Order in which automations run: This specific feature is not currently on our roadmap. While you can’t directly control the order automations run, you can achieve a similar outcome by combining actions into a single automation and arranging them in the desired sequence. Alternatively, you can use monday workflows (Enterprise only).
  2. Order of how automations appear: We’re looking into how we can improve the way automations are grouped and displayed in the automation center. This is part of our long-term roadmap, and we’re excited to explore how we can make automations easier to manage in the future! We will continue to keep you updated on any progress.

We appreciate your feedback as we continue to improve monday.com!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @amybronstein am I correct to assume that a topic which is marked as “solved” will be ignored for future considerations? Meaning the 170+ votes can be returned to users?

posts.author: Jit02 (Hello World)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com team,

Currently, the order of automations cannot be freely rearranged by users. This makes it difficult to keep things organized, especially when managing many similar or conditional automations. Over time, the automation list becomes messy and hard to follow.
It would be extremely helpful if users could manually rearrange the order of automations, for example, by dragging and dropping them to better align with workflows, priorities, or logic flow.

Hope this feature can be considered in future updates. Thank you!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi! Thank you for submitting your feature request! I wanted to point you to an existing feature request for this, here: Order of automations on a board - #68 by Katinal2.

Currently, this is not on the Product Roadmap. You can reference this January 2025 udpate from the monday team:

Order of automations on a board Feature requests
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team. Thank you for sharing your thoughts on the order of automations. We wanted to clarify the two main requests we’ve received here: Order in which automations run: This specific feature is not currently on our roadmap. While you can’t directly control the order automations run, you can achieve a similar outcome by combining actions into a single automation and arranging them in the desired sequence. Alternatively, you can use monday workflows (Enter…

If you’d like, I can help merge your feature request with the existing one to accurately count votes. This way, we can continue prioritizing the request with the Product team. Let me know if you’d like me to help with this!

posts.author: Jit02 (Hello World)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you so much for your detailed response!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: If this feature is “not on roadmap” what exactly does the priority option for automations do? I thought this would determine the order of execution.
